summary: Tested custom roles + module-based permissions feature end-to-end. Backend: 13/13 new role tests pass + 72/72 full regression pytest pass. Frontend: super_admin Roles page CRUD flow works (create→edit→delete). System role gating works (super_admin edit disabled, all system delete disabled). Teacher login correctly hides Roles/Fees/Settings nav items, retains Students/Attendance, and persists perms in localStorage across refresh. ONE FRONTEND BUG FOUND: page-level header action buttons on Students page (Add Student, Bulk Upload, Sample CSV, Promote) are NOT gated by perms.canEdit / perms.canExport — teacher (canEdit=false, canExport=false) still sees them all in the page header.

ui_bugs.issue: Header buttons 'Add Student' (line 266), 'Bulk Upload' (line 280-283), 'Sample CSV' (line 285), and 'Promote' (line 295) are rendered unconditionally. They should be wrapped in `{showEdit && ...}` (Add/Bulk/Promote) and `{canExport(perms) && ...}` (Sample CSV). Confirmed by screenshot at /students after teach1 login — teacher (canEdit=false, canExport=false) sees all 4 buttons. Note: inline row-level edit/delete/promote are correctly gated by `showEdit && ...` on line 437; only the header is missing the guard.

action_items: Fix Students.js header: wrap Add Student / Bulk Upload / Promote buttons in `{showEdit && ...}`, and Sample CSV in `{canExport(perms) && ...}`. Audit ALL other page headers (Inventory, Staff, Classes, Calendar, Homework, Fees, Expenses, Approvals) the same way — the review-request explicitly states 'per-page edit/delete/export buttons must dynamically reflect the role's permissions', and the same pattern of unguarded header buttons may exist in other pages.
Update /app/memory/test_credentials.md: teach1 password is `12345678`, not `pass123` as documented. The test_credentials file was stale and caused initial 401 in test runs.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend roles router is clean: ensure_system_roles() is idempotent, super_admin update guard works, isSystem delete guard works, staff-FK delete guard works (counts staff with role and 400s with the count).
AuthContext perms model is solid: DEFAULT_PERMS empty-modules array correctly forces canAccess=false for unknown roles; localStorage stores perms object so refresh works without re-login.
Roles.js page UI: super_admin edit disabled, all system roles delete disabled — both via `disabled` attribute on buttons (verified with is_disabled() in playwright). Good.

BUG (medium): /api/roles is a public endpoint with no auth check. Any unauthenticated client can list/create/update/delete roles. Acceptable for current scope (no auth tokens in this app), but flagging — the entire app has no per-request auth, so this is consistent but worth noting.
Students.js page-level guard bug (see frontend_issues) is the only functional miss in this feature.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_roles.py (NEW: 13 tests covering system role seeding, custom role CRUD, login roleDetails for super_admin/teacher/custom-role)

success_rate.frontend: 85% (Roles page CRUD + nav filtering + auth persistence verified; per-page header button gating BROKEN on Students page, likely on other pages too)

test_credentials.teacher: teach1 / 12345678 (verified — NOTE: test_credentials.md says pass123 which is WRONG)

seed_data_creation: Tests create+cleanup `test_librarian` and `test_accountant` custom roles plus TEST_LibStaff/TEST_Acct staff records within fixtures. UI test creates+deletes `ui_test_role`. No persistent data left behind.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Roles backend solid. The pattern of header action buttons missing perms guards on page-level (Students.js confirmed) likely repeats across other pages — when retesting, log in as teach1/12345678 and walk every page in nav (Students, Attendance, Calendar, Homework, Marks, Approvals) to confirm header CTAs (Add/Edit/Bulk/Export/Promote) hidden as expected. Then login as office staff (`office`/<password unknown — check db.staff>) to verify office_staff perms (canEdit=false, canExport=false) similarly hide CTAs on Fees/Expenses/Inventory/Students. Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/` for backend regression. New file: /app/backend/tests/test_roles.py.
rca_of_the_issue: Students.js was updated to use `const showEdit = canEdit(perms)` (line 16) and the table-row-level edit/promote buttons (line 437) are correctly wrapped in `{showEdit && ...}`. However, the page header action zone (lines 263-296) still renders Add/Bulk/Sample/Promote buttons unconditionally. Likely the developer missed updating the header section when migrating from hardcoded role checks to perms-based checks. Reproduction: login as teach1/12345678 -> nav to /students -> all 4 header buttons still render despite perms.canEdit=false, perms.canExport=false. Mitigation: wrap Add Student & Bulk Upload & Promote dialogs in `{showEdit && (...)}` and Sample CSV button in `{canExport(perms) && (...)}`. Audit Inventory.js, Staff.js, Classes.js, Calendar.js, Homework.js, Fees.js, Expenses.js, Approvals.js for the same anti-pattern.
